step_by_step_reasoning: We trace the architecture chronologically based on the whiteboard and the transcript. 1. Hospitals (UserConsumerHospital) upload clinical images to a raw storage S3 bucket. 2. Pathologists (UserCompanyDomainExpert) use an EKS-based platform to label and annotate these images. 3. SageMaker pulls raw data from S3, orchestrates training with FSx for Lustre for high-speed I/O and IAM for stringent security (HDS compliance). 4. The final model is saved to a separate output S3 bucket. 5. EKS pulls the model for pathologists to validate, then hosts it for real-time clinical inference accessed by hospitals.

graph.name: Kinaï Medical Image Analysis and Annotation Pipeline
graph.notes: Transitioned to SageMaker with FSx for Lustre, accelerating training by 30x and saving 30% on infrastructure costs. Compliant with HDS certification for sensitive health data.

nodes.name: Hospital / Clinicians
nodes.notes: Upload raw medical images and request inference.

nodes.name: Pathologists
nodes.notes: Perform data labeling, annotations, and final model validation.

nodes.name: Raw Medical Images
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores around 5TB of raw medical images per project.

nodes.name: Kubernetes labeling & inference platform
nodes.notes: Hosts labeling UI for pathologists and performs final model inference.

nodes.name: SageMaker Training
nodes.notes: Orchestrates parallel training pipelines containerized in Docker.

nodes.name: FSx for Lustre
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: High performance storage layer for accelerated training.

nodes.name: AWS IAM
nodes.notes: Ensures data access control to comply with HDS certification.

nodes.name: Model & Results Storage
nodes.notes: Stores exported models and pipeline execution results.

edges.notes: Hospitals upload raw medical images to S3.
Images are fetched from S3 for labeling.
Interactive labeling session returns results to pathologists.
Labeling platform triggers a new SageMaker training pipeline.
SageMaker streams training dataset from raw S3.
Uses FSx for fast I/O caching.
Verifies data access and compliance policies.
Saves trained model and output metrics.
Model results are loaded back to Kubernetes.
Pathologist views results to validate or reject the model.
Sends model validation approval back to platform.
Hospitals query EKS for inferences using the validated model.
Returns clinical analytics and prediction data back to the hospital.
